title: quassia orinocensis
intro: Quassia orinocensis, also known as Patino's Quassia, is a small tree or shrub that belongs to the family Simaroubaceae, native to the tropical regions of South America. The medicinal properties of Quassia orinocensis are attributed to its bark, which contains quassin, a lignan that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Quassia orinocensis include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Quassia orinocensis can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
